critical_issues: Projects CRUD broken: POST /api/projects returns 500 Internal Server Error. Root cause: database schema mismatch — the 'projects' table has column 'user_id' but ORM model defines 'owner_id'. All project CRUD operations fail (create, list by owner, get by id, update, delete all depend on owner_id).
Tasks CRUD broken: POST /api/tasks returns 422 (no project_id available because project creation fails). Cannot test Tasks CRUD without a project.

Browser CRUD tests could not be completed: browser agent API returned 401 invalid x-api-key (infrastructure issue — missing ANTHROPIC_API_KEY for browser agent service). Browser entity tests for projects, tasks, and login all show not_tested.

test_results.name: Projects: POST /api/projects (CREATE)
test_results.details: 500 Internal Server Error. DB column is 'user_id', ORM model uses 'owner_id'. SQLAlchemy cannot map the field.

test_results.name: Projects: GET /api/projects (LIST)
test_results.details: Cannot test — depends on project creation (query uses owner_id column).

test_results.name: Projects: GET /api/projects/{id} (READ)
test_results.details: Cannot test — no project ID available due to create failure.

test_results.name: Projects: PUT /api/projects/{id} (UPDATE)
test_results.details: Cannot test — no project ID available.

test_results.name: Projects: DELETE /api/projects/{id} (DELETE)
test_results.details: Cannot test — no project ID available.

test_results.name: Tasks: POST /api/tasks (CREATE)
test_results.details: 422 Unprocessable Entity — no project to link to.

test_results.name: Tasks: GET /api/tasks (LIST)
test_results.details: Cannot test without a valid project.

test_results.name: Browser smoke test: exit code 0 and overall=pass
test_results.details: qa-smoke-test.py exited 0, browser_smoke_test.overall=pass. Browser agent returned 401 (invalid API key) so CRUD tests could not be performed via browser — flagged as infrastructure issue.

database_schema_issue.description: The 'projects' table in the SQLite database has column 'user_id', but the SQLAlchemy ORM model (models.py) defines the column as 'owner_id'. This mismatch was introduced when the ORM model was updated but the DB was not migrated.
database_schema_issue.fix: Either: (1) rename DB column user_id to owner_id via migration, or (2) rename ORM field owner_id back to user_id. The DB was likely created before the ORM was updated.
